_comment: Synthetic H10b cross-situational-consistency DISCRIMINANT fixture (scoring.md §15.3). 40 participants across four channels (context / session / card_sort / predictions), a SHARED cohort keyed h10b-u00..h10b-u39. Ground truth: the person variability index V_i is drawn INDEPENDENT of [ level_i, aspirational gap_i, self-prediction cal_error_i ] (MAIN leg R² ~ 0 → upper 95% CI < 0.50), AND each cell's sd_i(c) is independent of |mbar_i(c)| (DE-CONFOUND R² ~ 0 → the within-person variability is not a mid-scale range artifact) — so H10b is SUPPORTED: cross-situational consistency is a DISTINCT construct (Fleeson 2001 / Mischel & Shoda 1995 / Doris 2002), not reducible to how high/aspirational/self-aware a person is. Consumed ONLY by analyze.py --h10b-log; isolated from the sample-context-log H10a/H10c cohort. Generated by /tmp/gen_h10b.py (self-checking, not committed).
